results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor itself to return or delete all personal data to the controller after processing ends. The contract instead requires the processor to render assistance to the controller to delete and/or return the controller’s data upon request. That is an assistance duty, not the processor’s own return/delete obligation, and it is narrower/different in substance.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: both require the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorisation; the contract parent clause also includes the notification duty and opportunity to object

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not describe the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in consulting supervisory authorities prior to processing, while the contract requires cooperation with the supervisory authority in performing its tasks

results.contract_source_text: to cooperate (including representatives of Processor), on the Controller’s request, with the supervisory authority in the performance of its tasks;

results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient align: the processor shall process personal data, matching the regulation's processor processing personal data duty

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object align: the regulation requires the processor to take all measures required under Article 32 / ensure security of processing, and the contract (read with the parent clause) requires the controller and processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk; this is the same security-measures obligation and is at least as strict
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are framed by the Article 32-style risk/state-of-the-art/cost/nature-scope-context-purpose assessment and risk to rights and freedoms
results.modality_check: Compatible: contract uses 'shall', equal force to the regulation's 'shall'

results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires including/ensuring confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services, and the contract sub-item expressly requires implementing measures with the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on taking into account the state of the art, implementation costs, processing nature/scope/context/purposes, and risk to rights and freedoms.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation's 'can' is satisfied by the contract's mandatory 'shall' obligation, which is at least as strong.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's manner/timing framing ('appropriate technical and organisational measures' to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including as appropriate) matches the regulatory security-measure context and is not weaker.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match the same obligation: the regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations to the controller, while the contract only gives the processor an entitlement, upon controller request, to satisfy a documentation request by referring the controller to sub-processor audit reports/documentation about sub-processor GDPR compliance. That is a narrower, different duty focused on sub-processor compliance documentation, not the processor's own Article 28 compliance information.

results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. This is the same general information-disclosure / audit-support obligation, and the contract provides a narrower but substantively aligned means of making compliance information available.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the compliance-demonstration context; the contract adds the controller-request condition, which is a reasonable and expected qualifier and does not make the duty weaker in substance.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall' for the obligation, and the 'entitled to fulfil' wording concerns the permitted method of performance, not a weakening of the underlying duty to provide the information/documentation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's reference to audit reports or similar documentation is an acceptable manner of making the necessary compliance information available; it is not looser than the regulation's requirement.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall not be liable for loss or damages if caused by the Controller’s failure to comply with its obligations according to applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulations or this Agreement. results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor not to engage a sub-processor unless it has prior specific or general written authorization from the controller; the contract instead requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors under a general written authorization regime, which is a different and narrower notice/objection mechanism rather than the authorization requirement itself.
results.condition_check: The contract is limited to the case of general written authorisation, which is only one branch of the regulation's 'specific or general written authorization' condition and does not cover specific written authorization.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall' for the notice duty, but that duty is not the same as the regulation's prohibition on engaging a sub-processor without prior authorization.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and manner differ materially: the regulation requires prior written authorization before engagement; the contract only requires informing the controller of intended changes and allows deemed acceptance after 30 days, which is not equivalent to prior authorization.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.condition_check: different condition: regulation is triggered by an instruction infringing GDPR or other data protection provisions; contract has no equivalent trigger and instead concerns legislative changes
results.modality_check: contract is mandatory but only for a different duty; it does not impose the required notification obligation

results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.modality_check: Compatible and stronger on the contract side: 'shall' is mandatory and exceeds regulatory 'can' only if the substantive duty matched, but here the substantive duty is different.
results.other_constraints_check: Not satisfied: the regulation requires regular testing/assessment/evaluation over time; the contract sub-item is about implementing a security capability, not regular testing or evaluation.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization. The contract parent clause expressly says the processor shall inform the controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of sub-processors, so the same obligation is present. The sub-item about deemed acceptance is additional and does not negate the informing duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the case of general written authorization / general written authorisation.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall', matching the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's 30-day deemed-acceptance mechanism is additional and not weaker than the regulation's timing requirement for informing; no conflicting manner, target, location, or cause constraints.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. results.contract_source_text: ‘Personal Data’ shall mean any information Processed by Processor in connection with the provision of the Processors’ Services under this Agreement relating to an identified or identifiable natural person ('Data Subject'); an identifiable person is one who can be identified, directly or indirectly, in particular by reference to an identifier such as a name, an identification number, location data, an online identifier or to one or more factors specific to his physical, physiological, genetic, mental, economic, cultural or social identity of that person;

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to impose Article 28(3) obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act. The contract clause instead concerns controller objection rights and notification of intended sub-processor changes; it does not impose the Article 28(3) obligations on sub-processors.
results.condition_check: The contract condition about the controller being unable to object if the new sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees is unrelated to the regulation's unconditional duty to impose obligations on sub-processors.
results.modality_check: The regulation requires a mandatory imposition obligation. The contract clause is about the controller's inability to object and does not impose the required duty; modality is not aligned.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation's manner requirement is by way of contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law. The contract clause does not deliver that obligation; it addresses a different procedural mechanism.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires that the controller have the right to object to intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors after being informed. The contract parent clause gives the processor a duty to inform the controller and expressly gives the controller the opportunity to object; the sub-item then deems changes accepted only if the controller does not object within 30 days. results.modality_check: Compatible and not weaker: the regulation's 'shall have the right to object' is met by the contract's express opportunity to object and the consequence that changes are accepted only absent objection within 30 days; this does not remove the right and is at least as strong in practical effect.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract requires notification by the processor and a 30-day window after notification, which is consistent with the regulation's requirement that the objection right arise after being informed of intended changes.

results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to take all measures required under Article 32 / ensure security of processing. The contract parent clause and sub-item address security measures and a process for testing, assessing, and evaluating their effectiveness, but the specific sub-item only points to measures described on an external website rather than itself imposing the full Article 32 obligation. The parent context does cover security measures generally, but the structured sub-item is a pointer to external details, not the full obligation.
results.condition_check: No condition on either side; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory. The contract sub-item is a plain statement and the parent context is mandatory in substance, but the sub-item itself is only descriptive and cross-references external security policies.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, target, location, or cause constraints that create a mismatch; however, the clause defers the actual measures to www.Processor.com, which is a pointer rather than in-line content.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: actor/action/object/recipient do not match: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority, while the contract clause is about remuneration for assistance and breach-attributable non-entitlement, not about assisting breach notification
results.condition_check: The contract condition (when the controller requires assistance under the agreement) is not the same triggering situation as assisting in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority
results.modality_check: The contract is a plain statement about remuneration/non-entitlement, not an obligation to assist
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing/manner/target/location/cause for the breach-notification assistance duty is provided
results.contract_source_text: In the event Controller requires assistance of Processor or the Processor’s Sub-processors pursuant to this Agreement (including, but not limited to clauses 5.3, 5.4, 5.5, 5.6, 5.7 and/or clause 7.2), this assistance is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3, which forms an integral part of the Agreement. Notwithstanding the before mentioned, Processor is not entitled to remuneration for circumstances which are attributable to Processor’s breach of security or Processor’s breach of it’s obligations as set out in this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires including/implementing organizational and technical measures to ensure security, and the contract requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security, including the cited confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience measure.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on taking into account state of the art, implementation costs, nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, and risk to rights and freedoms.
results.modality_check: Compatible: regulation uses 'can include' for examples within the security-measures requirement; the contract uses mandatory 'shall' to implement measures, which is at least as strong.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is at least as specific and protective, and the cited measure directly matches one of the regulation's listed security measures.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to include a substantive security measure—restoring availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident—whereas the contract merely points to Appendix 2 as the place where security-measure details are specified.
results.condition_check: No substantive condition match; the contract does not itself impose the incident-triggered restoration duty.
results.modality_check: The contract is only a pointer/statement of specification, not an obligation to perform the measure; it does not meet the required 'can/include' content obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The required timing ('in a timely manner') and incident context are absent from the contract clause itself; they are only potentially located elsewhere in Appendix 2, which is a pointer and not sufficient here.
results.contract_source_text: The details of the security measures taken by Processor in this respect of the Processing of Personal Data, are specified in Appendix 2, which forms an integral part of the Agreement.

results.contract_source_text: The process and timeframes for deletion of Controller’s data including the Personal Data is described in Appendix 4, which forms an integral part of the Agreement.

results.other_constraints_check: Timing is stricter on deletion/export, but the regulation's specific condition is after end of services; the contract context indicates this is the post-service deletion/export obligation. No contradictory weaker manner/location/cause constraints.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation gives the controller a right to object to changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors; the contract instead authorises the processor to transfer data to third-party integration service providers and to process returned data. That is a different obligation and does not address objection rights over sub-processor changes.
results.condition_check: Different condition: the contract is triggered by the controller using third-party integration services/applications, not by general written authorization or intended sub-processor changes after notice.
results.modality_check: Different modality and direction: the contract is an authorisation by the controller, not a mandatory controller right to object.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing or object scope; the clause concerns data transfers/processing for integrations, not objections after being informed of intended sub-processor changes.
results.contract_source_text: As part of the Main Agreement the Controller may also choose to use third party integration services and/or applications made available by the Processor on Processor.com in cooperation with the third party providers of such integration services and applications. If the Controller uses such third party integration services and/or applications it hereby authorises the Processor to: i) provide, transmit or transfer the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller to the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application, provided and only to the extent this is necessary for the performance and use by Controller of the said integration services and/or applications, and ii) Process data, including Personal Data, of the Controller that are transferred from the third party provider of the relevant integration service and/or application to the Controller’s Services with Processor.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections conducted by the controller or another auditor mandated by the controller. The contract sub-item, even with parent context, is about providing audit reports or similar documentation to document a sub-processor’s compliance, and charging extra audit reporting at the controller’s cost. That is a different obligation (documentation/reporting) rather than allowing/contributing to audits or inspections.
results.condition_check: The contract is triggered only if the Controller requests documentation of a Sub-Processor’s compliance; the regulation has no such condition. This is not the same audit/inspection duty.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'will' / 'is entitled to' language for additional audit reporting, which is not the same as a mandatory obligation to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections. Even considering the parent clause, the substance is permissive/conditional documentation handling, not the required audit-access duty.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract concerns audit reports/similar documentation and cost allocation, not the manner of allowing/contributing to audits or inspections by the controller or its mandated auditor.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;
